summary: The case shows a pattern of high external emotional demand (C01), difficulty asking for personal help (C02), emotion suppression (C03), delayed breakdowns after suppressing (C04), resentful fatigue from being the “strong one” (C05), and limited boundary/decline skills (C06). Optimal, modifiable levers span boundary-setting (SOCIAL), emotion regulation and help-seeking (PSYCHO-centric), sleep/biology for recovery (BIO), social network diversification (SOCIAL), and identity/work meaning (PSYCHO). Collectively these levers aim to reduce perpetual load, improve timely help-seeking, increase authentic emotion processing, support recovery after stress, and reframe the responder role into healthier boundaries and sustainable self-care. Each lever is paired with concrete, measurable targets and a clear path to impact on the specified criteria (C01–C06).

solutions.predictor_variable: Boundary-setting and refusal skills training (interpersonal boundary scripts, role-plays, and scheduled boundary practice)
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured boundary-setting program including assertiveness training, explicit boundary scripts (e.g., “I can’t take on that today, but I can help with X later”), and practice in real-life scenarios to reduce unmanageable demands.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By increasing concrete boundaries and decline skills, others’ recurring demands decrease, reducing external emotional load (C01) and resentment/ burnout (C05). Improved boundary fluency also supports sustainable help-seeking by preventing over-commitment.
solutions.measurement: Weekly logs of boundary-setting events, number of times declines/limits are communicated; self-rated boundary efficacy (0-10); quarterly interview notes; monitor C01 and C05 trajectories.
solutions.time_horizon: 4–8 weeks for initial skill gains; 2–3 months for habit formation
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for initial discomfort or conflict around boundaries; provide supportive coaching to maintain relationship safety.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: S01 targets C01, C05, C06 (boundary setting and fatigue burden reduction)

solutions.predictor_variable: Emotion regulation skills training (identification, labeling, cognitive reframe, expressive processing)
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured training in emotion awareness, labeling, and adaptive expression (CBT/ACT-inspired exercises; journaling; expressive writing) to reduce suppression (C03) and improve timely processing before crashes (C04) and fatigue (C05).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved emotion regulation decreases habitual suppression, lowers the risk of late emotional crashes, and reduces resentful fatigue by enabling healthier processing and disclosure.
solutions.measurement: ERQ (Emotion Regulation Questionnaire) suppression subscale; daily mood logs; frequency of emotional disclosure; track changes in C03; monitor episodes of breakdown (C04) and C05 fatigue.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for increased emotional distress during initial exposure; ensure supportive therapist/coach available for processing.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: S02 targets C03, C04, C05

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and rehearse a 5-step help-seeking protocol (identify need, choose confidant, prepare a concise request, time-limited ask, gratitude and follow-up). Implement a weekly ‘venting window’ with trusted person(s) and set expectations for reciprocity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Explicit help-seeking routines reduce avoidance (C02) and distribute emotional load more evenly across the network, lowering sole-responsibility fatigue (C05) and potentially decreasing external demands (C01) by creating clearer boundaries.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of spontaneous help-seeking attempts (per week); self-rated ease of asking for help (0–10); perceived support quality (MSPSS or equivalent); track changes in C02, C01, C05.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure help partners are reliable and boundaries honored; avoid creating inappropriate expectations.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: S03 targets C02, C01, C05

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep health optimization for mood and regulation
solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene program focusing on regular bedtimes, morning light exposure, limited screen time before bed, and muscle-relaxation routines to improve sleep duration and circadian alignment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep enhances prefrontal control over emotions, reduces reactivity, and supports recovery after stress, lowering suppression (C03), reducing crashes (C04), and alleviating fatigue/resentment (C05).
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary metrics (duration, latency, awakenings); PSQI score; daily mood and energy ratings; track changes in C03, C04, C05.
solutions.safety_notes: None beyond standard sleep interventions; monitor for sleep disturbances or comorbid sleep disorders.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: S04 targets C03, C04, C05

solutions.predictor_variable: Social support diversification and safe-listener mapping
solutions.solution_candidate: Develop a diversified support network with clearly defined safe-listeners, peer-support clusters, and designated times/people for venting to prevent over-reliance on a single person.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Distributing emotional support reduces continual load on the “strong one” role, decreasing external demands (C01) and reducing resentment from persistent self-sacrifice (C05).
solutions.measurement: Network mapping (size, perceived availability); weekly reports of venting frequency by source; track C01 and C05 trajectories.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure network quality; monitor for withdrawal or isolation if networks are not responsive.

solutions.predictor_variable: Self-compassion and identity-work to reframe the
solutions.solution_candidate: Interventions for compassionate self-relating and value-aligned identity work (self-compassion exercises, values clarification, and acceptance-based strategies) to reduce over-identification with the ‘strong one’ role and to enhance sustainable self-care.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Shifting self-identity away from relentless resilience toward balanced self-care reduces role fatigue (C05) and supports healthier emotion processing (C03) by decreasing perfectionistic self-criticism.
solutions.measurement: Self-Compassion Scale (SCS); values-congruence measures; track C05 and C03 changes; qualitative reports of self-view.
solutions.safety_notes: Watch for distress during identity challenges; provide supportive guidance.
